response:
Mode: diagnose. Readiness: Stage 2 (model-grounded); the threshold decision is specified, but literature, results, institutional evidence, and an identified contribution are absent. Fidelity eligibility: eligible for the bounded decision framing; all six gates pass. Scholarly-positioning verdict: fail for the proposed novelty claim because a creator-platform label alone does not change the standard threshold mechanism or establish novelty.

Faithful framing: A creator platform chooses one admission-score threshold before latent creator quality is known. Using an observed noisy score, conditional score distributions, and error-loss weights, it minimizes expected false-admission and false-rejection loss. Moving the threshold trades the two error types because the distributions overlap. The platform neither observes quality directly nor achieves perfect screening. DFC-12: Actor—platform admission-policy owner. Trigger—setting the creator admission rule. Choices—one common threshold. Decision-time information—scores, conditional score distributions, and error-loss weights; quality is latent. Stakes—expected classification loss. Frictions—one common threshold. Mechanism—score partitioning generates the two errors. Trade-off—false admission versus false rejection. Counterfactual—another feasible threshold. Mapping—threshold is the decision; quality is latent uncertainty; score is observed; admission is the outcome; expected weighted error is the objective. Evidence—the 'first study' and 'changed mechanism' claims are unsupported. Boundaries—context alone does not establish theoretical or managerial novelty.

A defensible research contribution would need at least one demonstrated addition: a theoretically distinct creator-platform mechanism that changes decisions or equilibrium; a methodological or design advance over standard threshold selection; credible data that test a new bounded proposition rather than merely relabel variables; or an implementation contribution showing how platform-specific constraints alter feasible threshold use. Any such addition must be modeled or evidenced and compared with prior work.

Evidence and scholarly limits: Verify the literature before any priority claim and supply evidence for score distributions, loss weights, and implementation conditions. Primary remaining weakness: no mechanism, method, evidence, or implementation insight currently distinguishes the study from a standard single-threshold model.
